prediction_ref.short_label: 1-bit native training becomes the dominant alternative to post-hoc quantization (led by Bonsai, Qwen/Llama 1-bit derivatives to follow)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Bonsai-8B 1-bit coverage continues with the same Medium / getdeploying / Firethering writeups (1.15 GB, 12-14x smaller, 6x faster on RTX 4090, llama.cpp + MLX support); no new lab ships a 1-bit-native checkpoint and no Qwen / Llama 1-bit derivative surfaces inside the Apr 21-23 window.

reference_links.label: Medium - New 1 bit LLM is here: Bonsai-8B

bridge.narrative: Bonsai-8B's continued reporting (1.15 GB, 12-14x smaller, 6x faster on RTX 4090, native llama.cpp / MLX support) keeps the precondition observation alive but the window adds no fresh 1-bit-native checkpoint and no Qwen or Llama 1-bit derivative, so the underlying claim continues without new acceleration.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a second lab to ship a 1-bit-from-scratch checkpoint or a Qwen / Llama 1-bit fork to confirm the trend.

prediction_ref.short_label: Convergence on an "Agent Registry" model (registry standard for distributing skills, tool permissions, audit traces)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Google launches Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with managed MCP server + A2A production release + 200+ Model Garden models, AWS Bedrock AgentCore ships Managed Harness + CLI + prebuilt skills for Kiro / Claude Code / Codex / Cursor, and OpenAI ships Workspace Agents — three vendor agent surfaces in one window — but no shared cross-vendor registry artifact format yet.

reference_links.label: Google Cloud - Introducing Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
AWS - Amazon Bedrock AgentCore adds new features
OpenAI - Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT

bridge.narrative: Google Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with managed MCP and A2A production, AWS Bedrock AgentCore's Managed Harness + CLI + prebuilt skills, and OpenAI Workspace Agents land together — three hyperscaler agent surfaces converging on the same lifecycle, audit, and skills story, sharpening the precondition for a registry standard but stopping short of a portable artifact spec.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a multi-vendor spec draft or a portable skill / tool-permission artifact format crossing AWS, Google, and OpenAI surfaces.

prediction_ref.short_label: Indirect prompt injection becomes a top CVE category (CVSS 8.0+ LLM-integration CVEs become Patch Tuesday regulars)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: The Apr 22 batch is broad-ecosystem (Xerte CVE-2026-34415 9.8, Esri ArcGIS CVE-2026-33519 9.8, OAuth2 Proxy CVE-2026-40575 9.1, Jellystat / EspoCRM / Nimiq) plus the Apr 22 governance push from Gemini Enterprise's AI Control Center, but no AI-integration codepath explicitly yields a CVSS 8.0+ entry inside the window.

reference_links.label: Google Cloud - The new Gemini Enterprise

bridge.narrative: The Apr 22 critical-CVE batch (Xerte 9.8, Esri ArcGIS 9.8, OAuth2 Proxy 9.1, Jellystat, EspoCRM, Nimiq) keeps Patch-Tuesday-grade severity flowing and Gemini Enterprise's AI Control Center frames indirect-injection / over-sharing as a governance category, but no AI-integration CVE explicitly lands inside this window.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need an explicit CVSS 8.0+ LLM-integration CVE inside the window or a confirmed Patch Tuesday rollup naming AI-integration codepaths.

prediction_ref.short_label: Acceleration of "local > cloud" inversion (by Q3 2026, individual / SMB coding agents standardize on local-default)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Qwen3.6-27B Apache 2.0 hits SWE-bench Verified 77.2% and Terminal-Bench 59.3% on a single RTX 4090 (~18 GB at Q4_K_M), beating the 397B MoE predecessor; Simon Willison rates it flagship-grade coding in 27B Dense and GIGAZINE notes Claude Opus 4.5-class performance is now downloadable for local execution.

bridge.narrative: Qwen3.6-27B Apache 2.0 hitting SWE-bench Verified 77.2% on a single RTX 4090 at ~18 GB Q4_K_M, with Simon Willison ranking it flagship-grade coding and GIGAZINE noting Claude Opus 4.5-class performance is now locally downloadable, directly materializes the precondition that local-runnable models clear the agent quality bar.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a major IDE or coding-agent vendor to ship a local-default config flag, not only a stronger local benchmark.

prediction_ref.short_label: Secret leakage through CI / agents becomes the next battleground ("Agent-in-the-Loop Secret Exfiltration" rises to top of CIS / OWASP risk)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: The Mythos Preview unauthorized-access incident hits day one (contractor credentials + Mercor data leak + naming-convention reconstruction); OAuth2 Proxy CVE-2026-40575 (CVSS 9.1, X-Forwarded-Uri auth bypass) discloses Apr 22 — both reinforce the agent-in-the-loop / supply-chain credential exfil shape but neither gets named as a CIS or OWASP category yet.

bridge.narrative: The Mythos Preview day-one breach via contractor credentials + Mercor leak + naming reconstruction, alongside OAuth2 Proxy CVE-2026-40575's X-Forwarded-Uri auth bypass, both materialize the agent-in-the-loop credential-exfil shape with concrete real-world incidents reinforcing the precondition.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need an explicit CIS or OWASP entry naming Agent-in-the-Loop Secret Exfiltration as a category, not only individual-incident reporting.

prediction_ref.short_label: "Headless Everything" architecture standardizes (headless-preferred metadata on MCP / Agent Registry)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Google's Project Mariner ships a web-browsing agent and OpenAI Workspace Agents emphasize cross-cutting Slack / Salesforce / files / code integrations rather than headless-preferred metadata; Google's managed MCP server and A2A production release move agent-to-agent plumbing forward without surfacing a headless metadata tag.

reference_links.label: TheNextWeb - Google Cloud Next 2026: AI agents, A2A, Workspace Studio

bridge.narrative: Today's headline agent moves are mixed: Workspace Agents lean into cross-tool integration and Project Mariner doubles down on browser-driving, while Google's managed MCP + A2A production release moves agent-to-agent plumbing forward without surfacing a headless-preferred metadata tag, leaving the registry-side prediction neither supported nor refuted.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a concrete MCP or agent-registry server metadata field carrying headless-preferred semantics.

prediction_ref.short_label: GGUF supply chains as new battleground (HF/Ollama/ModelScope standardize chat_template signature verification)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Qwen3.6-27B BF16 + FP8 weights drop on Hugging Face and ModelScope simultaneously and run via GGUF on llama.cpp b22099-series builds with no signature-verification gate at load time; the SGLang CVE-2026-5760 advisory continues but no hub announces default-on chat_template signing.

bridge.narrative: Qwen3.6-27B's simultaneous BF16 + FP8 drop on Hugging Face and ModelScope, with GGUF distribution running on llama.cpp without any signature-verification gate, keeps the structural fact alive that model artifacts ship executable templates, while the persistent SGLang CVE-2026-5760 advisory still has no hub-default mitigation answer.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need an explicit Hugging Face, Ollama Library, or vLLM / Bedrock policy switch turning on signature verification or SSTI scanning by default.

prediction_ref.short_label: The era of "exclusive alliance between hyperscaler and frontier lab" (5-pole regime locks in; Trainium / TPU / MAIA / MI300X design differences translate to model-behavior differences)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Anthropic confirms multi-GW next-gen TPU additions from 2027 via Google + Broadcom (Mizuho models $21B/$42B Broadcom revenue from Anthropic alone in 2026/2027) on top of the AWS Trainium commit, NVIDIA Vera Rubin's full 7-chip stack lands in mass production for H2 2026 hyperscaler rollout, and Anthropic's annualized revenue passes $30B — all crystallizing the codesigned-exclusive hyperscaler-lab-silicon shape.

reference_links.label: The Motley Fool - Anthropic Announcement for Alphabet and Broadcom
NVIDIA Newsroom - NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform

bridge.narrative: Anthropic's multi-GW TPU expansion via Google + Broadcom from 2027 (with Mizuho modeling $21B / $42B Broadcom revenue from Anthropic alone) atop the existing AWS Trainium commit, plus NVIDIA Vera Rubin's full 7-chip stack hitting mass production for H2 2026 hyperscaler rollout and Anthropic's annualized revenue clearing $30B, directly materialize the codesigned-exclusive precondition for the 5-pole regime.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need explicit lock-in for Meta-internal and xAI-Oracle/NVDA poles at the same scale plus a runtime-agnostic backend portability layer adopted at production scale.

prediction_ref.short_label: Proprietary re-expansion against an open-weight geopolitical split ("strongest model = closed weights" crystallizes)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Mythos Preview stays staged-gating closed (day-one breach reporting confirms third-party-only access) while Qwen3.6-27B's open-weight Apache 2.0 release reaches Claude Opus 4.5 / 4.6-class coding ability — confirming both halves of the split: closed for frontier / staged-gated, open weights settling at the 27-35B Dense production tier.

reference_links.label: Google Cloud - Introducing Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform

bridge.narrative: Mythos Preview's continued staged-gated third-party-only access pattern alongside Qwen3.6-27B Apache 2.0 reaching Claude Opus 4.5 / 4.6-class coding directly fits both halves of the structural split — closed weights for the frontier / staged tier, open weights consolidating at the 27-35B Dense production tier — strengthening the prediction's underlying force.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need a third frontier lab to publicly confirm an API-only flagship release before Q3 2026.

prediction_ref.short_label: "Training-vs-inference SKU split" becomes the standard for custom AI silicon

validation_rows.evidence_summary: NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform (Vera CPU + Rubin GPU + NVLink 6 + ConnectX-9 + BlueField-4 + Spectrum-6 Ethernet + Groq 3 LPU) lands as a 7-chip single-architecture stack covering both training and inference, sharpening the contrast against the hyperscaler dual-track SKU strategy without yet drawing MAIA / MTIA dual-SKU announcements.

bridge.narrative: NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform shipping a 7-chip single-architecture stack into mass production sharpens the structural contrast that the prediction draws — hyperscaler dual-track SKU on one side, NVIDIA single-architecture on the other — while keeping the underlying market split intact, even as MAIA / MTIA dual-SKU announcements remain pending.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need explicit Microsoft MAIA or Meta MTIA dual-SKU announcements adopting the training-vs-inference split.

prediction_ref.short_label: Physical AI as SaaS, "Robot-as-a-Service" infrastructure race (robot itself commoditizes; fleet management = recurring revenue)

validation_rows.evidence_summary: Hannover Messe 2026 (Apr 22-23) goes live with Agile Robots' Blue-Eye humanoid + the Accenture × Vodafone × SAP humanoid warehousing pilot; Tesla Optimus V3 confirms Fremont July-August production with 10M-unit Texas endgame; Toyota Woven City formalizes the Kakezan multi-AI rollout — all consistent with cloud-trained robot fleets as the recurring-revenue shape.

reference_links.label: BSS News - Agile Robots / German factories

bridge.narrative: Hannover Messe 2026 going live with Agile Robots' Blue-Eye and the Accenture × Vodafone × SAP humanoid warehousing pilot, Tesla Optimus V3's Fremont July-August production toward a 10M-unit Texas endgame, and Toyota Woven City's Kakezan multi-AI rollout all materialize the precondition that physical-AI fleets need cloud-trained policy delivery, reinforcing the RaaS shape.
bridge.remaining_gap: Need an explicit per-robot cloud-compute SKU and an enterprise contract template line item naming the metered RaaS charge.

prediction_ref.short_label: OAuth trust between AI SaaS becomes the top enterprise risk

validation_rows.evidence_summary: OAuth2 Proxy CVE-2026-40575 (CVSS 9.1, X-Forwarded-Uri auth bypass) discloses Apr 22; Google Workspace's Apr 22 Next 2026 announcement adds AI Control Center + Agent Management + Workspace Studio admin controls aimed at agent governance and over-sharing — first concrete admin-side moves toward the AI-extension scope category, even though no explicit AI-extension OAuth scope is named yet.
